output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Know your tenant rights and demand your money back in 60 seconds—no lawyer, no signup, just $19.99.
content_outline: The Real Reason Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (And Why They Get Away With It)
Can My Landlord Keep My Security Deposit? Know the Legal Deductions
How Long Is My Landlord Required to Return My Deposit by State
My Landlord Won't Give Me My Deposit Back: 3 Immediate Steps to Take
Why a Security Deposit Dispute Letter Works Better Than a Phone Call
The 60-Second Fix: Send a Demand Letter with No Sign-Up or Subscription
When to Escalate to Small Claims Court (and How Your Letter Helps)
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days; missing this deadline is often illegal.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear—only actual damage beyond ordinary use and unpaid rent.
A written demand creates a legal paper trail and frequently forces repayment before costly court action.
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate a professional deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99 with zero sign-up.
Documenting move-out photos, receipts, and state laws strengthens your case if the landlord continues withholding.
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord hold your money hostage. Create a professional deposit demand letter on Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no sign-up, no subscription, no stress.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who are problem-aware, actively searching for what to do when a landlord is withholding, keeping, or not returning a security deposit, and need an immediate, low-cost solution without hiring a lawyer.
